steps.title: Factor out the greatest common factor
steps.content:
Both terms share the factor $p^{17}$ (the lower power of $p$). Factor it out:
$$49p^{19}-121p^{17} = p^{17}(49p^2 - 121)$$

steps.title: Recognize the difference of squares pattern
steps.content:
The remaining expression $49p^2 - 121$ is a difference of squares. Notice $49p^2 = (7p)^2$ and $121 = 11^2$, so:
$$49p^2 - 121 = (7p)^2 - 11^2 = (7p - 11)(7p + 11)$$

steps.title: Write the complete factorization and identify the matching factor
steps.content:
Substituting back gives:
$$49p^{19}-121p^{17} = p^{17}(7p - 11)(7p + 11)$$
The factors of the original expression are $p^{17}$, $(7p - 11)$, and $(7p + 11)$. The expression $7p + 11$ appears as a factor, which matches choice A — A is correct.
